meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve no portfolio (Advanced Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no portfolio. A Advanced-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: User Onboarding to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to build advanced user onboarding to overcome a lack of portfolio. An expert-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code for students.
content.answer_block: Transform your blank GitHub profile into a high-value asset by building a sophisticated, data-driven user onboarding flow. By mastering state-machine logic and persistent user states, you demonstrate technical depth that separates you from beginners, proving you can ship production-ready features that actually drive user retention and engagement.
content.intro:
The silence of a recruiter looking at a thin portfolio is deafening. You possess the theoretical knowledge, but you lack the battle-tested evidence that you can build software that respects the user's journey. Most students build 'To-Do' lists; you are going to build a sophisticated onboarding engine that turns anonymous traffic into loyal power users.

We aren't just drawing UI elements; we are architecting a state-driven onboarding experience that tracks completion metrics and handles edge cases. By leveraging Antigravity for structural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative deployment, you will build a project that signals to employers that you understand the nuances of product engineering, not just syntax.

steps.title: Define the State Machine
steps.description: Establish the logical flow of your onboarding process to handle non-linear user paths. This prevents 'spaghetti code' and shows you think in systems.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a state machine schema for a 4-step SaaS onboarding flow including 'welcome', 'profile-setup', 'feature-tour', and 'dashboard-handoff'. Include logic for skipping steps.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'Create a TypeScript state machine using XState for the defined onboarding flow.'

steps.title: Implement Progress Persistence
steps.description: Ensure users never lose their progress by syncing state to a backend database. This demonstrates your ability to handle data integrity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Supabase integration service that saves the current onboarding step to a user profile table on every transition.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Initialize Supabase client and create a hook to update user_metadata in the database.'

steps.title: Build Context-Aware UI
steps.description: Develop UI components that react to the state machine's current step, ensuring the interface is always in sync with the backend data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate React components for the onboarding progress bar and step content wrapper using Tailwind CSS for a modern, enterprise look.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /edit 'Update the OnboardingWrapper component to render different views based on the current machine state.'

steps.title: Optimize for Analytics
steps.description: Add event tracking to your onboarding flow to measure drop-off points. This proves you understand business KPIs, not just code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an analytics middleware that fires events for 'onboarding_step_started' and 'onboarding_completed' to a dummy tracking endpoint.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'Implement a tracking utility that logs onboarding metrics to the console and a mock API endpoint.'

steps.title: Stress Test and Refactor
steps.description: Use Claude Code to identify potential race conditions or state synchronization bugs during rapid user navigation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the onboarding logic for potential state collisions when a user navigates back and forth rapidly.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /test 'Run unit tests on the onboarding state machine to ensure valid transitions and error handling.'

pro_tips: Always wrap your onboarding in a Try-Catch block that triggers a fallback UI so the user isn't stuck on a blank screen if the DB fails.
Use optimistic UI updates to make the onboarding feel instantaneous, even if the backend sync is delayed.
Document your decision-making process in a 'Decisions.md' file in your repo; recruiters love seeing how you weighed trade-offs.
Add a 'skip' button that saves the user's current progress; forced onboarding is the fastest way to lose users.
Use CSS transitions for step changes to add a 'polished' feel that makes your app look significantly more expensive than it was to build.
content.next_steps: Deploy your app to Vercel, record a 60-second Loom video explaining the state machine architecture, and link it directly at the top of your resume.
content.conversion_block: You now have a production-grade feature that demonstrates system architecture, database management, and UI/UX design—all in one project. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
